title: tulipa karatavica
intro: Tulipa karatavica, also known as the wild tulip, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the lily family. It is a bulbous plant with red or yellow flowers and is native to the Balkans and Asia Minor. The medicinal properties of Tulipa karatavica include its use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Tulipa karatavica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing respiratory issues.
